interpretation: Each node contains a normalized key element and all atomic facts associated with that element.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 001

interpretation: An edge links two key-element nodes when each element appears in the other's associated atomic facts.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 002

interpretation: Atomic facts preserve a coarse compressed view while chunk identifiers link the graph back to detailed original text.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 003

interpretation: A per-agent notebook accumulates supporting facts and functions as the working representation used for final reasoning.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 004

interpretation: Split a long document into paragraph-preserving chunks bounded by the LLM context limit.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 005

interpretation: Prompt the LLM to convert each chunk into indivisible atomic facts and extract essential nouns, verbs, and adjectives as key elements.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 006

interpretation: Normalize key elements to reduce lexical noise and granularity variation.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 007

interpretation: Associate each normalized element with its source atomic facts and link pairs whose elements are reciprocally mentioned.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 008

interpretation: Key-element nodes are coarse navigation and initial-selection units.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 009

interpretation: Atomic-fact groups provide a compact overview and help the agent choose which original chunks merit reading.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 010

interpretation: Original chunks and their previous or subsequent neighbors provide fine-grained evidence.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 011

interpretation: Neighboring graph nodes enable long-range and multi-hop traversal beyond adjacent text.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 012

interpretation: Independent agent notebooks are consolidated as the evidence units for final answer reasoning.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 013

interpretation: Create a step-by-step rational plan and select several initial key-element nodes based on the question and plan.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 014

interpretation: Explore coarse-to-fine: inspect atomic facts, queue promising original chunks, optionally read adjacent chunks, and then traverse graph neighbors.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 015

interpretation: Use explicit agent functions for reading chunks, searching more, traversing neighbors, or terminating once evidence is sufficient.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 016

interpretation: Run multiple searches from independent initial nodes, then combine their notes and use chain-of-thought plus majority voting to resolve inconsistencies.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 017

interpretation: The graph externalizes long-range associations between details that may occur far apart in the source document.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 018

interpretation: Atomic facts compress details for navigation, while on-demand chunk access restores source detail before a claim enters the notebook.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 019

interpretation: Agent reflection continually updates a notebook and changes subsequent exploration actions.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 020

interpretation: Final synthesis combines independent notebooks, complementary evidence, and majority voting before answer generation.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 021

interpretation: Complex multi-hop question answering over long input documents.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 022

interpretation: Single-hop long-document question answering where the answer detail may be hard to locate.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 023

interpretation: Questions requiring long-range dependencies among facts distributed through contexts from 16k to 256k tokens.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 024

interpretation: Repeated questions over one document are identified as an efficient use case because the graph can be reused.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 025

interpretation: Multi-hop QA on HotpotQA, 2WikiMultiHopQA, MuSiQue, and HotpotWikiQA-mixup.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 026

interpretation: Single-hop long-context QA on NarrativeQA.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 027

interpretation: Evaluate HotpotWikiQA-mixup at 16k, 32k, 64k, 128k, and 256k input lengths.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 028

interpretation: Use exact match, F1, optimized F1 star, and two LLM-rater correctness measures.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 029

interpretation: Ablate the rational plan and reasoned node selection, and vary the initial-node count and chunk size.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 030

interpretation: Using a 4k working context, GraphReader reports the strongest scores among evaluated systems across the four LongBench datasets.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 031

interpretation: GraphReader remains substantially stronger than baselines from 16k through 256k on HotpotWikiQA-mixup, while full-context GPT-4 degrades with length.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 032

interpretation: Reasoned node selection is important: random selection causes an average 18 percent decline in the ablation.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 033

interpretation: The final notebook recalls more supporting evidence than the initial atomic-fact layer, suggesting that on-demand chunk reading adds useful detail.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 034

interpretation: A graph can be reused across multiple questions about the same document, amortizing its construction cost.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 035

interpretation: The implementation relies on a closed GPT-4 API and therefore may face rate and regional restrictions.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 036

interpretation: Agent efficiency depends on the underlying model's planning and reasoning capabilities.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 037

interpretation: Too many initial nodes introduce noise, especially for single-hop questions.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 038

interpretation: Chunks larger than the reported optimum can hide details, while smaller chunks cause semantic truncation.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 039

interpretation: The paper evaluates fixed long documents and does not report an incremental document-graph update procedure.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 040

interpretation: The standard configuration uses a 4k agent context, maximum 2k-token chunks, five initial nodes, and at most ten function calls per search path.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 041

interpretation: GraphReader consumes 52.8k tokens per 358.3k-token question context versus 48.7k for ReadAgent in the reported 256k benchmark analysis.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 042

interpretation: The graph-construction cost can be amortized in single-document, multiple-query scenarios.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 043

interpretation: No dynamic update benchmark is presented; the graph is constructed from the input document before exploration.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 044

interpretation: The authors argue that trees, pagination, and simple retrieval do not adequately expose long-range multi-hop dependencies in very long text.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 045

interpretation: GraphReader extracts atomic facts and key elements from chunks, normalizes the elements, and connects mutually referring element nodes.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 046

interpretation: A rational plan decomposes the question before the agent selects initial graph nodes.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 047

interpretation: Exploration is coarse-to-fine, moving from key-element nodes to atomic facts, original chunks, adjacent chunks, and neighboring nodes.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 048

interpretation: The agent uses reflection and explicit functions to decide whether to read more evidence, traverse, or terminate.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 049

interpretation: Independent search notebooks are combined with chain-of-thought and majority voting for final answer generation.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 050

interpretation: The evaluation spans single-hop and multi-hop QA with source lengths reaching 256k tokens.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 051

interpretation: GraphReader with a 4k working context outperforms the reported GPT-4-128k baselines across the tested LongBench tasks.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 052

interpretation: On HotpotWikiQA-mixup, GraphReader degrades much less than full-context GPT-4 as input length grows to 256k.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 053

interpretation: Removing the rational plan hurts performance, while random node selection causes a much larger average decline.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 054

interpretation: Five initial nodes and 2k-token chunks are empirical optima; more nodes add noise and either much larger or smaller chunks lose information.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 055

interpretation: GraphReader uses only modestly more tokens than ReadAgent in the reported 256k analysis but achieves much stronger task scores.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 056

interpretation: The final notebook has higher supporting-fact recall than the atomic-fact representation, indicating gains from active exploration.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 057

interpretation: Closed-model dependency and the agent's reliance on planning quality are the paper's explicit limitations.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 058

interpretation: GraphReader is an agentic reading method for a long input, not a precomputed cross-corpus global summarizer; its retrieval policy is adaptive and question-specific.
title: 2406.14550v2 retrieval-strategy claim 059
